title: The Motel Where the Shadow Left

features.name: the still-turning fan
features.desc-on-touch: Three blades revolving. They cast a shadow on the wall — but you notice the shadow stopped moving years ago. It holds the shape the fan made in 1987 and does not update.

features.name: the shadow-shape
features.desc-on-touch: A silhouette on the linoleum that does not match the fan above it. It moves to a rhythm from a different evening. The archive wonders if the shadow stayed when the motel's warmth left.

features.name: the key-counter
features.desc-on-touch: Wood stained with thirty-eight years of dusk. The register is open but holds no numbers, only dates that loop. When you read them, you are already here in 1987.

features.name: the room-where-the-photo-was
features.desc-on-touch: A doorway to a room that contains the motel as it was the moment the photograph was taken. You can see it twice: as it was and as it is, compressed into one space.

features.name: the guest-absence
features.desc-on-touch: A chair, a bed, a shape of weight on fabric. But the person who made the shape is elsewhere now, probably reading this archive, probably thinking about the shadow that wouldn't follow the fan.

inscription: the fan remembers when it turned and cast a shadow, and the shadow learned to stay while the fan moved on

file.name: Photo_Motel_Fan_Shadow_1987.jpg
file.note: Motel room at dusk. Fan in background. The shadow on the wall behind it is sharper than the photograph's resolution should allow. ERROR: photograph contains the present-moment motel simultaneously wit

exits.label: back to the service station where the light is fading
into the corridor where each room is a year
through the wall where shadows live when they separate
